meta_title: For Students: SEO for SaaS to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SEO for SaaS and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SEO for SaaS to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to master SEO for SaaS and solve your no-portfolio crisis. A high-impact guide using Antigravity and Claude Code for intermediate builders.
content.answer_block: To solve the no-portfolio problem, build a high-performance SEO-optimized SaaS landing page. Use Antigravity to generate high-authority content structures and Claude Code to implement technical SEO standards. By demonstrating real-world ranking architecture, you transform a simple project into a professional asset that proves your technical expertise to recruiters.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the ultimate enemy. You have the skills, but your GitHub is a graveyard of tutorials and your portfolio is non-existent. Employers don't want to see another To-Do list app; they want to see someone who understands how to build products that actually get found in a crowded marketplace.

This case study shifts your focus from merely 'writing code' to 'engineering growth.' By mastering SEO for SaaS, you bridge the gap between a student developer and a product-focused engineer. We will use modern AI agents to build a high-converting, search-engine-ready architecture that serves as the centerpiece of your professional portfolio.

steps.title: Architecting the SEO Schema
steps.description: Define the semantic structure of your SaaS to ensure search engines understand your value proposition immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a JSON-LD schema for a SaaS product focusing on [SaaS Niche]. Include Organization, Product, and FAQ schema types optimized for high SERP visibility.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --file schema.json --context 'Implement this JSON-LD schema into the root layout of our Next.js project.'

steps.title: Automated Content Strategy
steps.description: Use AI to generate long-tail keyword clusters that your SaaS will target to attract organic traffic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Identify 10 low-competition, high-intent long-tail keywords for a [SaaS Niche] app. Map these to potential blog post titles and meta descriptions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write --file content_strategy.md --content 'Generate the content map based on the Antigravity keyword analysis.'

steps.title: Technical SEO Implementation
steps.description: Optimize your page speed and accessibility, which are core ranking factors for modern SaaS platforms.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a checklist for optimizing Next.js Core Web Vitals, specifically focusing on LCP and CLS for a SaaS landing page.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --command 'npm run lint && npm run build' --fix-errors

steps.title: Dynamic Meta Tag Injection
steps.description: Implement server-side rendering for dynamic meta tags to ensure every sub-page is crawlable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a utility function for a Next.js App Router to dynamically generate title and description tags based on database content.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --file src/lib/seo.ts --logic 'Implement dynamic meta tag generation based on the provided utility.'

steps.title: Analytics and Conversion Tracking
steps.description: Set up tracking to prove your SaaS performance to potential employers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a configuration guide for implementing Plausible Analytics with custom event tracking for SaaS CTA clicks.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --file lib/analytics.ts --context 'Integrate Plausible tracking with event listeners for button clicks.'

pro_tips: Prioritize 'Long-tail' keywords over high-volume terms to show recruiters you understand niche acquisition strategy.
Always include a 'Build Log' in your portfolio that links your GitHub commits to your SEO performance metrics.
Implement OpenGraph tags for every page; it demonstrates you understand social distribution, not just search.
Use 'Semantic HTML' headers (H1-H6) strictly for hierarchy, not for styling, to satisfy search crawlers.
Showcase your 'Lighthouse Score' on your portfolio site; it is the industry standard for proving technical competence.
content.next_steps: Deploy your project to Vercel, run a Lighthouse audit, and link the live site directly in your LinkedIn featured section to demonstrate real-world shipping capability.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for the perfect job and start building the proof of your talent. Your portfolio is your resume, your code is your reputation. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
